name: Hillary Clinton
subject: a note about the last few days
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hillary for America, a grassroots campaign of 1
body:
Now more than ever, we need love and kindness | Friend --

Like so many people across America, I have been following the news of the past few days with horror and grief.

On Tuesday, Alton Sterling, father of five, was killed in Baton Rouge -- approached by the police for selling CDs outside a convenience store. On Wednesday, Philando Castile, 32 years old, was killed outside Minneapolis -- pulled over by the police for a broken tail light.

And last night in Dallas, during a peaceful protest related to those killings, a sniper targeted police officers -- five have died: Brent Thompson, Patrick Zamarripa, Michael Krol, Michael Smith, and Lorne Ahrens. Their names, too, will be written on our hearts.

What can one say about events like these? It’s hard to know where to start. For now, let’s focus on what we already know, deep in our hearts: There is something wrong in our country.

There is too much violence, too much hate, too much senseless killing, too many people dead who shouldn’t be. No one has all the answers. We have to find them together. Indeed, that is the only way we _can_ find them.

Let’s begin with something simple but vital: listening to each other.

White Americans need to do a better job of listening when African Americans talk about seen and unseen barriers faced daily. We need to try, as best we can, to walk in one another’s shoes. To imagine what it would be like if people followed us around stores, or locked their car doors when we walked past, or if every time our children went to play in the park, or just to the store to buy iced tea and Skittles, we said a prayer: “Please God, don’t let anything happen to my baby.”

Let’s also put ourselves in the shoes of police officers, kissing their kids and spouses goodbye every day and heading off to do a dangerous job we need them to do. Remember what those officers in Dallas were doing when they died: They were protecting a peaceful march. When gunfire broke out and everyone ran to safety, the police officers ran the other way -- _into_ the gunfire. That’s the kind of courage our police and first responders show all across America.

We need to ask ourselves every single day: What can I do to stop violence and promote justice? How can I show that your life matters -- that we have a stake in another’s safety and well-being?

Elie Wiesel once said that “the opposite of love is not hate -- it’s indifference. And the opposite of life is not death -- it’s indifference.”

None of us can afford to be indifferent toward each other -- not now, not ever. We have a lot of work to do, and we don’t have a moment to lose. People are crying out for criminal justice reform. People are also crying out for relief from gun violence. The families of the lost are trying to tell us. We need to listen. We need to act.

I know that, just by saying all these things together, I may upset some people.

I’m talking about criminal justice reform the day after a horrific attack on police officers. I’m talking about courageous, honorable police officers just a few days after officer-involved killings in Louisiana and Minnesota. I’m bringing up guns in a country where merely talking about comprehensive background checks, limits on assault weapons and the size of ammunition clips gets you demonized.

But all these things can be true at once.

We do need police and criminal justice reforms, to save lives and make sure all Americans are treated as equal in rights and dignity.

We do need to support police departments and stand up for the men and women who put their lives on the line every day to protect us.

We do need to reduce gun violence.

We may disagree about how, but surely we can all agree with those basic premises. Surely this week showed us how true they are.

I’ve been thinking today about a passage from Scripture that means a great deal to me -- maybe you know it, too:

_“Let us not grow weary in doing good, for in due season, we shall reap, if we do not lose heart.”_

There is good work for us to do, to find a path ahead for all God’s children. There are lost lives to redeem and bright futures to claim. We must not lose heart.

May the memory of those we’ve lost light our way toward the future our children deserve.

Thank you,

Hillary

***

_Learn more about Hillary’s plans to tackle criminal justice reform:[https://www.hillaryclinton.com/issues/criminal-justice-reform/](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=11&ms=MjU5NDE5NjQS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODQxMjM5Nzk2S0&mt=1&rt=0)

Learn more about Hillary’s plans to prevent gun violence: [https://www.hillaryclinton.com/issues/gun-violence-prevention/](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=6&ms=MjU5NDE5NjQS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODQxMjM5Nzk2S0&mt=1&rt=0)_

| | Paid for by Hillary for America, a grassroots campaign of 1.3 million donors committed to electing Hillary Clinton (and keeping Donald Trump out of the White House).
---
Contributions or gifts to Hillary for America are not tax deductible.
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. This is your campaign, so if you have thoughts on anything at all, just [click here](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=9&ms=MjU5NDE5NjQS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODQxMjM5Nzk2S0&mt=1&rt=0) to send us a message! If you'd like more information on key policies, visit [hillaryclinton.com/issues](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MjU5NDE5NjQS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODQxMjM5Nzk2S0&mt=1&rt=0). Getting emails from Hillary for America is one of the best ways to stay in touch with this campaign, but if you really want to scale back, click [here](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=5&ms=MjU5NDE5NjQS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODQxMjM5Nzk2S0&mt=1&rt=0) to receive less email and click [here](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=1&ms=MjU5NDE5NjQS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODQxMjM5Nzk2S0&mt=1&rt=0) to unsubscribe. There are other ways to stay involved -- be sure to follow the campaign on [Facebook](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=3&ms=MjU5NDE5NjQS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODQxMjM5Nzk2S0&mt=1&rt=0), [Twitter](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=8&ms=MjU5NDE5NjQS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODQxMjM5Nzk2S0&mt=1&rt=0), and [Instagram](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=12&ms=MjU5NDE5NjQS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODQxMjM5Nzk2S0&mt=1&rt=0). Puedes encontrar más información sobre la campaña de Hillary en español [aquí](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=4&ms=MjU5NDE5NjQS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODQxMjM5Nzk2S0&mt=1&rt=0). If you're still reading this, you must be a really dedicated Hillary supporter. [Yee-haw for you](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=10&ms=MjU5NDE5NjQS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODQxMjM5Nzk2S0&mt=1&rt=0)! Thanks so much for doing everything you can to elect Hillary Clinton as our next president.
Hillary for America, P.O. Box 5256, New York, NY 10185
[Privacy Policy](http://links.hillaryclinton.com/ctt?kn=7&ms=MjU5NDE5NjQS1&r=MTA3MzIzMDg3NjIwS0&b=0&j=ODQxMjM5Nzk2S0&mt=1&rt=0)

name: Team Gregg
subject: Have you seen these headlines?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gregg for Indiana
body:
After reading headlines like these, it's no wonder Governor Pence is considering bailing out.
| [](https://act.myngp.com/el/1563727192750688256/2347923019101767680?utm_campaign=fnd_hedvid_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=gregg&refcode=em16_160708_fnd_gq_hedvid_ful__tg&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=Peter&lastname=)
---
Peter, The facts are clear: Governor Pence is failing Indiana and Hoosier working families are paying the price. But you don't have to take our word for it. **[We've released a new video that lets the latest headlines state the truth about Governor Pence's failed leadership in black and white. Watch the video now.](https://act.myngp.com/el/1563727192750688256/2347923019101767680?utm_campaign=fnd_hedvid_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=gregg&refcode=em16_160708_fnd_gq_hedvid_ful__tg&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=Peter&lastname=)** [](https://act.myngp.com/el/1563727192750688256/2347923019101767680?utm_campaign=fnd_hedvid_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=gregg&refcode=em16_160708_fnd_gq_hedvid_ful__tg&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=Peter&lastname=) From RFRA to disastrous jobs plans, it's no wonder Indiana is still lagging behind the rest of the country. Governor Pence has failed Indiana families, and if Donald Trump wants him, he can have him. **[Watch the video now.](https://act.myngp.com/el/1563727192750688256/2347923019101767680?utm_campaign=fnd_hedvid_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=gregg&refcode=em16_160708_fnd_gq_hedvid_ful__tg&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=Peter&lastname=)** Thanks for your support, Team Gregg
| | [**Watch the video ->**](https://act.myngp.com/el/1563727192750688256/2347923019101767680?utm_campaign=fnd_hedvid_&utm_medium=email&utm_source=gregg&refcode=em16_160708_fnd_gq_hedvid_ful__tg&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=Peter&lastname=)

name: Team Russ
subject: FWD: Your page is loading.
disclaimer_text: paid for by Russ for Wisconsin, Joe Sensenbrenner, Madison, WI, Treasurer
body:
We can have faster broadband. | Friend, Did Russ's last email load for you? Maybe slow internet could have thwarted your efforts to sign. We're not sure, but it's a depressingly possible explanation, so we wanted to make sure you had another chance. **[Click here to demand faster broadband internet by classifying it as a public utility -- like electricity.](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf439f/1c469400/61b9afd/356d490/3073414530/VEsH/ "http://action.russfeingold.com/page/s/160707-broadband?utm_source=ema_2016-07-08_broadband-ns&source=ema_2016-07-08_broadband-ns&utm_campaign=broadband&utm_medium=ema")** Thanks for signing today. Team Russ | [ADD YOUR NAME »](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf439f/1c469400/61b9afd/356d490/3073414530/VEsE/ "http://action.russfeingold.com/page/s/160707-broadband?utm_source=ema_2016-07-08_broadband-ns&source=ema_2016-07-08_broadband-ns&utm_campaign=broadband&utm_medium=ema")
---

\---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: Russ Feingold
Date: Thu, Jul 7, 2016
Subject: Your page is loading
[](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf439f/1c469400/61b9afd/356d490/3073414530/VEsF/ "http://action.russfeingold.com/page/s/160707-broadband?utm_source=ema_2016-07-08_broadband-ns&source=ema_2016-07-08_broadband-ns&utm_campaign=broadband&utm_medium=ema")
Friend,
Does this look familiar to you?
[](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf439f/1c469400/61b9afd/356d490/3073414530/VEsC/ "http://action.russfeingold.com/page/s/160707-broadband?utm_source=ema_2016-07-08_broadband-ns&source=ema_2016-07-08_broadband-ns&utm_campaign=broadband&utm_medium=ema")
As I've met with people across rural Wisconsin, one thing has become abundantly clear: We need better, faster broadband internet.
But my opponent, Senator Johnson, is choosing to listen to the needs of companies like Charter, Time Warner Cable, and AT&T -- not to the needs of Wisconsinites. In fact, Senator Johnson was one of six senators to sign a letter to the FCC urging the commission to slow things down, and define broadband internet at just 4 mbps, down from the new and improved standard of 25 mbps. That reclassification would help Charter and Time Warner make money, but it would hurt students and businesses across Wisconsin.
**[Let's treat broadband internet as a utility like we do electricity, and work to provide high internet speeds to all areas of the state. Sign your name here if you agree.](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf439f/1c469400/61b9afd/356d490/3073414530/VEsD/ "http://action.russfeingold.com/page/s/160707-broadband?utm_source=ema_2016-07-08_broadband-ns&source=ema_2016-07-08_broadband-ns&utm_campaign=broadband&utm_medium=ema")**
Right now, broadband service is often expensive, slow, and sometimes only offered by one company with no other option. This problem is even worse in rural areas, where broadband is usually more expensive and slower than in urban areas. Yet as I listen to Wisconsinites across the state, it's rural Wisconsinites who are telling me about the fundamental educational and economic necessity of broadband.
I've heard from students whose home internet service is so spotty that, late at night, they'll go sit in the parking of the local library to get WiFi signal -- just so they can send in a job application or finish a test. For small business owners, slow broadband can quite literally result in lost revenue, or prevent significant growth.
This is a no-brainer for anyone who isn't taking orders from giant telecom corporations: We need to strengthen our rural economies by giving them the tools to succeed and thrive in a 21st century economy. We can start by treating broadband as the essential economic engine that it is.
**[Add your name here to support treating broadband as a public utility like electricity.](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf439f/1c469400/61b9afd/356d490/3073414530/VEsA/ "http://action.russfeingold.com/page/s/160707-broadband?utm_source=ema_2016-07-08_broadband-ns&source=ema_2016-07-08_broadband-ns&utm_campaign=broadband&utm_medium=ema")**
Sincerely,
Russ Feingold
[ADD YOUR NAME »](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf439f/1c469400/61b9afd/356d490/3073414530/VEsB/ "http://action.russfeingold.com/page/s/160707-broadband?utm_source=ema_2016-07-08_broadband-ns&source=ema_2016-07-08_broadband-ns&utm_campaign=broadband&utm_medium=ema")
---

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you would like to make a contribution to Russ for Wisconsin, [click here.](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf439f/1c469400/61b9afd/356d491/3073414530/VEsO/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/rdf-def?utm_source=ema_2016-01-11_advocacy-footer-contribute&refcode=ema_2016-01-11_advocacy-footer-contribute&utm_campaign=advocacy-footer-contribute&utm_medium=ema") If you would like to receive fewer emails, [click here](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf439f/1c469400/61b9afd/356d48e/3073414530/VEsP/ "http://action.russfeingold.com/page/s/advocacy-optout"). If you would like to change your email or salutation, [click here](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf439f/1c469400/61b9afd/356d48f/3073414530/VEsHBQ/ "http://action.russfeingold.com/page/s/changeinfo"). [Click here to unsubscribe](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf439f/1c469400/61b9afd/356d48c/3073414530/VEsHBA/ "http://action.russfeingold.com/unsubscribe"). Contributions or gifts to Russ for Wisconsin are not tax deductible. | Authorized and paid for by Russ for Wisconsin, Joe Sensenbrenner, Madison, WI, Treasurer

name: Team Katie
subject: a broken system
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Katie McGinty for Senate |
body:
Friend,

Six years ago, when the Supreme Court ruled on Citizen’s United, the floodgates of special interest money were cracked wide open, flooding our political system.

The impact has been devastating: Today — and during our own race right now — unaccountable dark money from the ultra-rich and huge corporations is threatening to drown out the voices of everyday, hardworking Americans.

We need to raise our voices to DEMAND lawmakers fix our failing campaign finance system. Add your name immediately:

http://sign.actiontaker.org/page/s/ECU-We-The-People-S?source=mcginty

It’s infuriating, but our system is broken, and the few who can afford it are spending unlimited sums of dark money to elect partisan politicians they can keep in their pocket.

We cannot stay quiet, change will not come by waiting on the sidelines. Change will come when we give Congress no other choice but to act.

Stop dark money from buying our elections. Sign your name now: http://sign.actiontaker.org/page/s/ECU-We-The-People-S?source=mcginty

Time to fight back,

Team Katie

* A note on donations *

Katie is fighting for working families in PA — but we’re up against major special interest spending. We’re relying on grassroots donations from people like you to win this race and take back the Senate.
Chip in $5 or more today. Thank you.

Donate Now: https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/elect-katie-mcginty?refcode=e20160709&refcode2=e20160709&amount=25

----------------------------------------------
| Paid For By Katie McGinty for Senate |
----------------------------------------------

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
If you no longer wish to receive updates from our campaign, please https://act.myngp.com/el/6903265192142962688/-5884491673857163264.

Katie McGinty for Senate
PO Box 22447
Philadelphia PA 19110 United States

name: staff@maggiehassan.com
subject: Link of the Week: Big win for transgender rights
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
_This is our Link of the Week email -- the things that we're sharing around the office, and we think everyone on Team Maggie should know about. We hope you'll enjoy clicking around as much as we do._
Last week, Governor Hassan signed an executive order banning discrimination based on gender identity and expression in the New Hampshire state government. This is a big win for gender equality in the Granite State, and we're so proud to work for a leader who understands that our society is stronger when every individual is fully included in civic and economic life.
But our country still has a long way to go -- Too many states still don't have protections in place for the rights of transgender state workers. A national survey of transgender Americans found that 1 in 4 transgender workers have lost a job due to anti-trans bias, and _half_ have been harassed at work.
**[Read more about Governor Hassan's work to ensure equality in New Hampshire.](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fd5e6/2a380d31/10f81327/fbb33a/2527065962/VEsH/ "http://www.concordmonitor.com/Hassan-bans-discrimination-based-on-gender-identity-3172020")**
-- Team Maggie
P.S. -- Polls show this race is still neck-and-neck. **[Chip in $3 right now to help elect Maggie Hassan and send a fighter for gender equality to the Senate.](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fd5e6/2a380d31/10f81327/fbb33b/2527065962/VEsE/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiaGFzc2FuX3Nlbl9lbWFpbCIsImUzc2djbVZtWTI5a1pTQjlmUT09IjoiZTIwMTYwNzA5X2xvdHdfRnVsbCIsImUzc2dURkJCUVZGMVpYSjVVM1J5YVc1bklIMTkiOiIzLDI1LDUwLDEwMCwyNTAsNTAwIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/hassan_sen_email?refcode=e20160709_lotw_Full&amounts=3,25,50,100,250,500")**

clean_body: This is our Link of the Week email -- the things that we're sharing around the office, and we think everyone on Team Maggie should know about. We hope you'll enjoy clicking around as much as we do. Last week, Governor Hassan signed an executive order banning discrimination based on gender identity and expression in the New Hampshire state government. This is a big win for gender equality in the Granite State, and we're so proud to work for a leader who understands that our society is stronger when every individual is fully included in civic and economic life. But our country still has a long way to go -- Too many states still don't have protections in place for the rights of transgender state workers. A national survey of transgender Americans found that 1 in 4 transgender workers have lost a job due to anti-trans bias, and half have been harassed at work. "" -- Team Maggie P.S. -- Polls show this race is still neck-and-neck. "" ** "" **

body:
[](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fd5e5/2a380d31/10ff1190/fbb064/4265490316/VEsH/ "http://www.maggiehassan.com/")
[](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fd5e5/2a380d31/10ff1190/fbb064/4265490316/VEsE/ "http://www.maggiehassan.com/")
Hey there -- Wanted to make sure you knew that we've extended our Polling Fund deadline for 24 hours. We fell just short of our goal last night, and we have to keep pushing.
Our race in New Hampshire is THE race for Senate control, and the latest polls show that we're locked in a dead heat -- we can ONLY shore up these numbers with help from grassroots supporters like you.
With the future of the Supreme Court on the line, your gift now, before tonight's deadline, couldn't be more critical. [**Take your support for electing Governor Hassan and taking back the Senate to the next level by donating $3 or whatever you can before tonight's deadline. Every dollar you can give makes a difference.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fd5e5/2a380d31/10ff1190/fbb065/4265490316/VEsF/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiaGFzc2FuX3Nlbl9lbWFpbCIsImUzc2djbVZtWTI5a1pTQjlmUT09IjoiZTIwMTYwNzA5X3BvbGxzMS1LaWNrZXJfbmgtbmQiLCJlM3NnVEZCQlFWRjFaWEo1VTNSeWFXNW5JSDE5IjoiMywyNSw1MCwxMDAsMjUwLDUwMCJ9/ "http://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/hassan_sen_email?refcode=e20160709_polls1-Kicker_nh-nd&amounts=3,25,50,100,250,500")
Thanks for stepping up,

Marc Goldberg
Campaign Manager, Maggie for NH
_\-----------Original Message-----------_
On Wednesday, July 6, at 2:21 PM, Team Maggie
<info@maggiehassan.com> wrote:
Here's where things stand -- Our race in New Hampshire will likely determine Senate control and the future of the Supreme Court, and the polls are WAY too close for comfort.
**Governor Hassan had a small lead, but a recent poll shows that after misleading ads from her corporate special interest backers went on air, Washington Republican Kelly Ayotte is closing in:**
[](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fd5e5/2a380d31/10ff1190/fbb066/4265490316/VEsC/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiaGFzc2FuX3Nlbl9lbWFpbCIsImUzc2djbVZtWTI5a1pTQjlmUT09IjoiZTIwMTYwNzA5X3BvbGxzMS1LaWNrZXJfbmgtbmQiLCJlM3NnVEZCQlFWRjFaWEo1VTNSeWFXNW5JSDE5IjoiMywyNSw1MCwxMDAsMjUwLDUwMCJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/hassan_sen_email?refcode=e20160709_polls1-Kicker_nh-nd&amounts=3,25,50,100,250,500")
The best way for us to bounce back is by putting $30,000 in our Polling Fund by Friday at midnight. With so much on the line, we HAVE to regain our momentum. [**Please, make your support official and donate $3 or more right now so we can get back on track to take back the Senate and secure the future of the Supreme Court.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fd5e5/2a380d31/10ff1190/fbb065/4265490316/VEsD/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiaGFzc2FuX3Nlbl9lbWFpbCIsImUzc2djbVZtWTI5a1pTQjlmUT09IjoiZTIwMTYwNzA5X3BvbGxzMS1LaWNrZXJfbmgtbmQiLCJlM3NnVEZCQlFWRjFaWEo1VTNSeWFXNW5JSDE5IjoiMywyNSw1MCwxMDAsMjUwLDUwMCJ9/ "http://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/hassan_sen_email?refcode=e20160709_polls1-Kicker_nh-nd&amounts=3,25,50,100,250,500")
